steps.title: Fit the two charge points
steps.content: The answer choices are linear functions of the number of lessons, so the fastest check is to fit the two given data points. Enter $(6,150)$ and $(17,480)$ in Desmos as $x_1=[6,17]$ and $y_1=[150,480]$, then run $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=30$ and $b=-30$, so the model is $f(x)=30x-30$.

steps.title: Interpret the regression
steps.content: The slope $30$ means each additional lesson adds 30 dollars. The intercept $-30$ is not a literal fee here; it is the intercept of the simplified linear rule for $x>2$. The graph still has to pass through the two real charge points, and $y=30x-30$ does.

steps.title: Write $f(x)$
steps.content: Use the fitted equation as the function: $f(x)=30x-30$. Check the two given points: $f(6)=30(6)-30=150$ and $f(17)=30(17)-30=480$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A) $f(x) = 25x$: $f(6) = 150$ but $f(17) = 425 e 480$.
  • B) $f(x) = 25x + 55$: $f(6) = 205 e 150$.
  • C) $f(x) = 30x$: $f(6) = 180 e 150$.
  • D) $f(x) = 30x - 30$: $f(6) = 150$ and $f(17) = 480$, both match.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
